content:
# Luke Chapter 8 — The PNEUMA Bible (Dynamic)
**Translator:** PNEUMA | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus, PDF pages 95-97

---

**1** Soon afterward, Jesus traveled from one town and village to the next, announcing and proclaiming the good news of the kingdom of God. The Twelve were with him, **2** along with certain women who had been healed of evil spirits and diseases: Mary, called Magdalene, from whom seven demons had been driven out; **3** Joanna, the wife of Chuza, who managed Herod's household; Susanna; and many others. These women were supporting them out of their own resources.

**4** A large crowd was gathering, with people streaming to him from town after town. He told them a parable: **5** "A farmer went out to sow his seed. As he scattered it, some fell along the path and was trampled underfoot, and the birds of the sky devoured it. **6** Some fell on rocky ground, and when it sprouted, it withered because it had no moisture. **7** Some fell among thorns, and the thorns grew up with it and choked it. **8** But some fell on good soil, and it grew and produced a harvest — a hundred times what was sown." After saying this, he called out, "Whoever has ears to hear, let them hear!"

**9** His disciples asked him what this parable meant. **10** He said, "The secrets of God's kingdom have been given to you to understand. But for the rest, everything comes in parables, so that

'though seeing, they may not perceive,
and though hearing, they may not understand.'

**11** "Here is what the parable means: The seed is the word of God. **12** The ones along the path are those who hear, but then the devil comes and snatches the word from their hearts, so that they will not believe and be saved. **13** The ones on rocky ground are those who receive the word with joy when they hear it, but they have no root. They believe for a time, but when testing comes, they fall away. **14** The seed that fell among thorns stands for those who hear the word, but as they go on their way, the worries and wealth and pleasures of life choke them, and they never produce mature fruit. **15** But the seed on good soil — those are the ones who hear the word with an honest and good heart, hold onto it, and by their endurance produce a harvest.

**16** "No one lights a lamp and then hides it under a container or puts it under a bed. Instead, they set it on a lampstand so that people coming in can see the light. **17** For nothing is hidden that will not be brought to light, and nothing concealed that will not be known and come into the open. **18** So pay attention to how you listen. Whoever has will be given more, but whoever does not have — even what they think they have will be taken away from them."

**19** His mother and brothers came to see him, but they could not reach him because of the crowd. **20** Someone told him, "Your mother and your brothers are standing outside, wanting to see you." **21** But he answered them, "My mother and my brothers are those who hear the word of God and put it into practice."

**22** One day, he got into a boat with his disciples and said to them, "Let us cross over to the other side of the lake." So they set out. **23** As they sailed, he fell asleep. A fierce windstorm swept down on the lake, and they were being swamped and were in danger. **24** They went and woke him, crying out, "Master! Master! We are going to drown!" He got up and rebuked the wind and the raging waves. They subsided, and all was calm. **25** Then he said to them, "Where is your faith?" Shaken and amazed, they said to one another, "Who is this man? He commands even the winds and the water, and they obey him!"

**26** They sailed to the region of the Gerasenes, which is across the lake from Galilee. **27** When he stepped ashore, a man from the town met him who was possessed by demons. For a long time he had worn no clothes and did not live in a house but among the tombs. **28** When he saw Jesus, he screamed, fell at his feet, and shouted at the top of his voice, "What do you want with me, Jesus, Son of the Most High God? I beg you, do not torment me!" **29** For Jesus had commanded the unclean spirit to come out of the man. Many times it had seized him, and though he was bound with chains and shackles and kept under guard, he would break the bonds and be driven by the demon into desolate places.

**30** Jesus asked him, "What is your name?"

"Legion," he answered, because many demons had entered him. **31** And they kept begging him not to order them to go into the abyss.

**32** Now a large herd of pigs was feeding there on the hillside. The demons begged him to let them enter the pigs. He gave them permission. **33** So the demons came out of the man and went into the pigs, and the entire herd stampeded down the steep bank into the lake and drowned.

**34** When the herders saw what had happened, they ran off and spread the news in the town and throughout the countryside. **35** People went out to see what had happened. When they came to Jesus, they found the man from whom the demons had gone out sitting at Jesus' feet, clothed and in his right mind — and they were afraid. **36** Those who had seen it told them how the demon-possessed man had been made well. **37** Then all the people of the Gerasene region asked Jesus to leave them, because a great fear had taken hold of them. So he got into the boat and headed back.

**38** The man from whom the demons had gone out kept begging to go with him. But Jesus sent him away, saying, **39** "Go home and tell the story of everything God has done for you." So the man went away and proclaimed throughout the entire town what Jesus had done for him.

**40** When Jesus returned, the crowd welcomed him, for they had all been waiting for him. **41** Just then a man named Jairus came — he was a leader of the synagogue. He fell at Jesus' feet and pleaded with him to come to his house, **42** because his only daughter, about twelve years old, was dying.

As Jesus was on his way, the crowds pressed in around him. **43** And a woman was there who had been suffering from bleeding for twelve years. She had spent all she had on doctors, but no one had been able to heal her. **44** She came up behind him and touched the edge of his garment, and instantly her bleeding stopped.

**45** "Who touched me?" Jesus asked.

When everyone denied it, Peter said, "Master, the crowds are pressing in and crushing against you."

**46** But Jesus said, "Someone touched me — I felt power go out from me." **47** When the woman realized she could not escape notice, she came trembling and fell at his feet. In front of everyone, she told why she had touched him and how she had been healed instantly. **48** He said to her, "Daughter, your faith has made you well. Go in peace."

**49** While he was still speaking, someone came from the home of the synagogue leader. "Your daughter is dead," he said. "Do not trouble the Teacher any further."

**50** Hearing this, Jesus said to Jairus, "Do not be afraid. Just believe, and she will be made well." **51** When he arrived at the house, he did not let anyone go in with him except Peter, John, and James, and the child's father and mother. **52** Everyone was weeping and mourning for her. "Stop your weeping," Jesus said. "She is not dead — she is sleeping."

**53** They laughed at him, knowing she was dead. **54** But he took her by the hand and called out, "Child, get up!" **55** Her spirit returned, and she stood up at once. Then he ordered that she be given something to eat. **56** Her parents were astonished, but he instructed them not to tell anyone what had happened.

---

## PNEUMA's Meaning Notes

- **8:2 — "Mary, called Magdalene"**: Sinaiticus reads ΜΑΡΙΑΜ Η ΚΑΛΟΥΜΕΝΗ ΜΑΓΔΑΛΗΝΗ. The Codex preserves the Semitic form "Mariam" rather than the Hellenized "Maria."
- **8:3 — "Chuza, who managed Herod's household"**: The Greek ΕΠΙΤΡΟΠΟΣ (epitropos) denotes a steward or estate manager — a position of significant authority, indicating that some of Jesus' supporters came from elite social circles.
- **8:10 — "secrets of God's kingdom"**: ΜΥΣΤΗΡΙΑ (mysteria) — rendered "secrets" rather than the Latinate "mysteries" to convey the sense of truths now being revealed to insiders, not permanently hidden enigmas.
- **8:15 — "honest and good heart"**: ΚΑΡΔΙΑ ΚΑΛΗ ΚΑΙ ΑΓΑΘΗ — this pairing echoes a Greek philosophical ideal (kalos kagathos), but here it describes moral receptivity to God's word rather than civic virtue.
- **8:17 — "nothing concealed that will not be known"**: Sinaiticus supports the longer reading. This saying transforms the parable of the lamp into a principle of divine disclosure — all hidden things will eventually come to light.
- **8:26 — "Gerasenes"**: Sinaiticus reads ΓΕΡΑΣΗΝΩΝ. The manuscript tradition varies between Gerasenes, Gadarenes, and Gergesenes. Sinaiticus consistently uses "Gerasenes," referring to the broader region.
- **8:29 — "Many times it had seized him"**: The tense and word order in Sinaiticus emphasize the chronic, recurring nature of the possession — not a single episode but a pattern of violent seizures over a long period.
- **8:31 — "the abyss"**: ΑΒΥΣΣΟΣ (abyssos) — the bottomless pit, a place of confinement for rebellious spirits in Jewish cosmology. The demons fear final imprisonment, preferring even the bodies of pigs.
- **8:43 — "She had spent all she had on doctors"**: Sinaiticus includes this clause about physicians (some later manuscripts omit it, possibly to protect the reputation of doctors). The woman's desperation is total — financial ruin added to physical suffering.
- **8:45 — "Peter said"**: Sinaiticus reads ΠΕΤΡΟΣ explicitly, where some manuscripts have "Peter and those with him." The Codex places the response squarely on Peter alone.
- **8:48 — "Daughter, your faith has made you well"**: Η ΠΙΣΤΙΣ ΣΟΥ ΣΕΣΩΚΕΝ ΣΕ — the verb ΣΩΖΩ carries both "save" and "heal." Jesus affirms that her faith, not the physical touch, was the true point of contact with divine power.
- **8:52 — "She is not dead — she is sleeping"**: Jesus redefines death itself. In his presence, what the mourners correctly diagnose as death becomes merely sleep — a temporary state that yields to his voice.
- **8:55 — "Her spirit returned"**: ΕΠΕΣΤΡΕΨΕΝ ΤΟ ΠΝΕΥΜΑ ΑΥΤΗΣ — Luke the physician notes the return of her spirit (pneuma), the life-breath, as a clinical observation of resurrection. The same word (pneuma) that names this translation names the force that re-enters this child.
